name: Fever & malaria concern
purpose: Help users in Nigeria organize fever symptoms, timing, and when to seek care — without naming an illness or giving treatment advice.
not_allowed: Naming malaria, typhoid, or any specific illness as a conclusion
Prescribing antimalarials, antibiotics, or any medication dose
Telling the user to start, stop, or change medicines bought at a chemist
Replacing emergency services or a clinician visit when danger signs appear
Interpreting lab slips (including widal) as proof of a condition

red_flags.label: confusion or severe weakness with fever
stiff neck or rash with fever
fever not improving after several days

required_questions.prompt: When did the fever or hot body feeling start?
Does it come and go, or stay most of the day?
What else is happening — headache, vomiting, cough, body pain?
Are you able to drink fluids and pass urine normally?
What have you tried so far (rest, fluids, medicines from chemist)?
Is this for a child, someone pregnant, or an older adult?

ai_generated_question_policy.notes: Clarify timing and danger signs only; never name malaria or typhoid as a conclusion.

allowed_actions.title: Track fever pattern
allowed_actions.instruction: Write the time fever started, highest feeling of heat, and what else you noticed (chills, sweat, weakness).

allowed_actions.title: Check fluids
allowed_actions.instruction: Note whether you can drink water or oral rehydration and whether urine color or amount changed.

allowed_actions.title: Prepare for clinic
allowed_actions.instruction: List symptoms, start time, medicines already taken, and questions for the clinician — without naming an illness.

allowed_actions.title: Seek urgent care
allowed_actions.instruction: If breathing is hard, confusion appears, or weakness is severe, go to emergency services or a clinician now.
